← Minh Tran

Creating Computer Games for Learning

University of Chicago Pre-College Program — Summer 2026

Instructor: Minh Tran   Role: Instructor of Record

Dates: July 7–23, 2026   Format: In-person (some sessions via Zoom)

Course Description

This course explores the design and development of educational computer games. Students learn Python programming alongside theories of learning and game design, culminating in an original educational game built for a self-chosen target audience.

Lecture Schedule

Week Day Date Lecture Lab
Week 1 Tue 7/7 Part 1: Introduction & what is an educational game? Part 2 (Python): Introducing Python and Variables Coding Practice (individual); Design Exercise: Defining your mythical users (individual)
Wed 7/8 Part 1: Constructivism & flow theory; Part 2 (Python): Turtles and statements Coding Practice (individual); Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #1: Finalize group members
Thu 7/9 Part 1: ZPD, scaffolding, and learning trajectories; Part 2: Functions and Conditionals Coding Practice (individual); Open work time (group)
Fri 7/10 Part 1: Game Elements; Part 2: Iteration (iTiCSE) Coding Practice (individual); Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #2: Target Users and Learning Content
Week 2 Mon (Zoom) 7/13 Part 1: Game Elements (cont.) & Game Design Frameworks; Part 2: Strings, lists, and event handling Coding Practice (individual); Open work time (group)
Tue (Zoom) 7/14 Part 1: Classes and Objects; Part 2: Guest Speaker (TBD) Coding Practice (individual); Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #3: Basic Game Design
Wed (Zoom) 7/15 Part 1: Lingering biases & Inclusive Design (CRP, UDL); Part 2: Classes and Objects (cont.) & Debugging strategies (Field trip) Weston Game Lab
Thu 7/16 Part 1: Motivation & Assessment; Part 2: Introducing Pygame Coding Practice (individual): Setting up Pygame; Open work time (group)
Fri 7/17 (Students + Lecture) Part 1: Flash Talk and Peer Review Workshop; Part 2: Exploring Pygame Coding Practice (individual): Mini game (Bubble Pop); Open work time (group)
Week 3 Mon 7/20 Part 1: Guest Speaker (TBD); Part 2: Panel Discussion Coding Practice (individual): Mini game (Memory Tiles); Open work time (group)
[Assignment Due] Project Milestone #4: Final Game Design
Tue 7/21 AI in Games: Challenges and Opportunities Coding Practice (individual): Mini game (Maze Navigator); Open Work Time (group)
Wed 7/22 (Movie Time) Free Guys Open Work Time (group)
Thu 7/23 (Students) Project Presentation ("Final Exam") (no class)
[Extra Credit] Project Milestone #5: MVP Implementation

Milestones

Milestone Description Due
#1 Finalize group members Wed 7/8
#2 Target Users and Learning Content Fri 7/10
#3 Basic Game Design Tue 7/14
#4 Final Game Design Mon 7/20
#5 (Extra Credit) MVP Implementation Thu 7/23
